audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for census, birth in Bethlehem, manger, shepherd announcement, angelic peace, circumcision/name, temple presentation, Simeon, Anna, Nazareth return, and twelve-year-old Jesus.

verses.restored: In those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the inhabited world should be registered.
This first registration happened while Quirinius was governing Syria.
All went to be registered, each to his own city.
Joseph also went up from Galilee, from the city Nazareth, to Judea, to David's city, which is called Bethlehem, because he was from the house and family line of David,
to be registered with Mary, who had been betrothed to him, being pregnant.
While they were there, the days were fulfilled for her to give birth.
She gave birth to her firstborn son, wrapped him in cloths, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guest room.
In the same region there were shepherds living in the fields, keeping watch over their flock by night.
A messenger of the Lord stood over them, and glory of the Lord shone around them, and they feared a great fear.
The messenger said to them, Do not fear, for look, I announce to you good news of great joy that will be for all the people.
Today in David's city a Savior was born for you, who is Messiah, Lord.
This will be the sign for you: you will find a baby wrapped in cloths and lying in a manger.
Suddenly there was with the messenger a multitude of the heavenly army praising the Divine and saying,
Glory to the Divine in the highest, and on earth peace among people of favor.

When the messengers went away from them into heaven, the shepherds were saying to one another, Let us go through to Bethlehem and see this word/event that has happened, which the Lord made known to us.
They went with haste and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
Seeing, they made known the word spoken to them concerning this child.
All who heard marveled concerning the things spoken by the shepherds to them.
But Mary kept all these words/events, pondering them in her heart.
The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising the Divine for all they heard and saw, just as it had been spoken to them.
When eight days were fulfilled to circumcise him, his name was called Jesus, the name called by the messenger before he was conceived in the womb.
When the days of their purification according to the Law of Moses were fulfilled, they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord,
as it has been written in the Law of the Lord: Every male opening the womb shall be called holy to the Lord,
and to give a sacrifice according to what is said in the Law of the Lord: a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons.
Look, there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon; this man was righteous and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel, and holy Spirit was upon him.
It had been revealed to him by the holy Spirit that he would not see death before he saw the Lord's Messiah.
He came in the Spirit into the temple; and when the parents brought in the child Jesus to do for him according to the custom of the Law,
he received him into his arms, blessed the Divine, and said,
Now you release your servant, Master, according to your word, in peace,
because my eyes have seen your salvation,
which you prepared before the face of all peoples,
a light for revelation to the nations and glory of your people Israel.
His father and mother were marveling at the things spoken concerning him.
Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, Look, this one is appointed for falling and rising of many in Israel, and for a sign spoken against,
and a sword will pass through your own soul also, so that reasonings from many hearts may be revealed.
There was Anna, a prophetess, daughter of Phanuel, from the tribe of Asher; she was advanced in many days, having lived with a husband seven years from her virginity,
and she was a widow until eighty-four years, who did not depart from the temple, serving with fastings and prayers night and day.
At that very hour she stood near, gave thanks to the Divine, and spoke about him to all those waiting for redemption of Jerusalem.
When they completed all things according to the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own city Nazareth.
The child grew and became strong, being filled with wisdom; and favor of the Divine was upon him.
His parents went each year to Jerusalem for the feast of Passover.
When he became twelve years old, they went up according to the custom of the feast.
After completing the days, as they were returning, the boy Jesus remained behind in Jerusalem, and his parents did not know it.
Supposing him to be in the travel group, they went a day's journey and were seeking him among relatives and acquaintances.
Not finding him, they returned to Jerusalem seeking him.
After three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the teachers, hearing them and questioning them.
All those hearing him were astonished at his understanding and answers.
Seeing him, they were struck with wonder, and his mother said to him, Child, why did you do this to us? Look, your father and I were distressed, seeking you.
He said to them, Why were you seeking me? Did you not know that it is necessary for me to be in the things/house of my Father?
They did not understand the word that he spoke to them.
He went down with them and came to Nazareth, and was subject to them. His mother kept all these words/events in her heart.
Jesus advanced in wisdom and stature, and in favor with the Divine and humans.
